findings.summary: Commodore LCD replica/interface board. CR-prefixed diodes misclassified as capacitors, speaker and DC-DC converter misclassified, no regulators detected.

correct.description: All ICs correctly identified and classified
LM324 and LM358 opamps correctly identified

incorrect.description: 12 CR-prefixed diodes misclassified as capacitor (CR is a standard diode reference designator per IEEE 315)
CNP1 speaker classified as capacitor instead of speaker/transducer
CN2 DB37 connector classified as mounting_hole instead of connector
DC1 DCDC converter classified as diode instead of power converter

missed.description: No power regulators detected despite DC-DC converter and voltage regulation on board

suggestions: Recognize CR prefix as diode (IEEE 315 standard reference designator for diodes)
Classify Speaker_Crystal and similar speaker library symbols as transducer not capacitor
Classify DB-series connectors (DB9, DB25, DB37) as connector not mounting_hole
Classify DC-prefix and DCDC-Conv library parts as power converter
Detect DC-DC converters as power regulators from component type and net connections

findings.summary: Commodore LCD computer (231 components). Correct: all ICs, 44 ferrite beads, crystals, UART, opamps, bus topology. Incorrect: 12 CR-prefixed diodes misclassified as capacitor (CR is standard diode prefix), Speaker CNP1 as capacitor, DB37 connector CN2 as mounting_hole, DC1 DCDC-Conv as diode. Missed: no power_regulators detected, 4N28 optocoupler not in isolation_barriers.

correct.description: 44 FB-prefixed components correctly classified as ferrite_bead
Crystal circuits Y1 4MHz and Y3 3.579545MHz correctly detected

incorrect.description: 12 CR-prefixed components misclassified as capacitor instead of diode (CR1 1N4728 zener, CR15 LM385-2.5 voltage reference, etc)

missed.description: No power_regulators detected despite DC1 DCDC-Conv generating -12V/-5V

suggestions: Add CR as standard diode reference prefix
Add DCDC/DC-DC value patterns to ic classification
